2606.08324.title: Set-Based Transformer for Atmospheric Compensation in Standoff LWIR Hyperspectral Imaging
2606.08324.paper_summary: Passive long-wave infrared (LWIR) hyperspectral imaging under a standoff geometry depends on atmospheric absorption and emission, as well as reflected radiance, thus making atmospheric compensation essential to get knowledge of a target of interest. Despite its importance, this compensation has been largely overlooked due to its practical and modeling difficulty. In this paper, we present a lightweight set-based deep learning framework that takes multiple radiance measurements, collected at different standoff ranges, as input and jointly estimates transmittance, atmospheric path radiance, and a shared downwelling spectrum. We analyze the learned representation with a sparse autoencoder and observe that several latent features do activate on geographically coherent subsets of the test data despite the absence of location supervision. Experiments on a MODTRAN generated standoff LWIR dataset demonstrate low spectral distortion across all estimated products. The dataset and code is publicly available at: https://factral.co/SAE-LWIR/
2606.08324.paper_summary_zh: 被動長波紅外（LWIR）高光譜成像在遠距幾何下依賴於大氣的吸收和發射，以及反射輻射，因此大氣補償對於獲取感興趣目標的知識至關重要。儘管其重要性，這一補償在實際和建模上都面臨困難，因而在很大程度上被忽視。在本文中，我們提出了一個輕量級基於集合的深度學習框架，該框架將在不同遠距範圍內收集的多個輻射測量作為輸入，並共同估計透過率、大氣路徑輻射和共享的下行光譜。我們使用稀疏自編碼器分析學習到的表示，觀察到儘管缺乏位置監督，但幾個潛在特徵確實在測試數據的地理一致子集上激活。對MODTRAN生成的遠距LWIR數據集的實驗顯示，所有估計產品的光譜失真均較低。該數據集和代碼可在以下網址公開獲取：https://factral.co/SAE-LWIR/
2606.08324.author: Fabian Perez et.al.
2606.08324.authors: Fabian Perez, Nicolas Quintero, Jeferson Acevedo, Hoover Rueda-Chacon
